content: To support "silent-on-error" derives, you'll need to depend on the nightly trivial_bounds feature. Is this something you'd want to do? If so, we can prioritize developing support for silent derives now; otherwise, you can start using zerocopy by only applying derives where they are applicable.
If you add for<'a> to the bound, that silences the trivial bounds error. It works even if no lifetimes appear in the where clause.
We really try to avoid relying on accidental compiler loopholes in zerocopy.
would it be okay if it's behind the --cfg zerocopy_unstable (or another flag)?
It might be a loophole, but given its usage in pin-project and pin-init it is impossible for compile to break this use case now :)
Y'all are very convincing. :-) We'll have derive(zerocopy_derive::most_traits) in our next release under --cfg zerocopy_unstable_linux: https://github.com/google/zerocopy/pull/3416
